pattern: Sort characters by frequency descending and assign in frequency order to key slots; the i-th most-frequent character (0-indexed) occupies slot i, costing floor(i/8)+1 pushes per occurrence.
hints: Count the frequency of each letter in word; there are at most 26 distinct ones and 8 keys.
Sort frequencies in descending order; assign the highest-frequency character to slot 0 (costs 1 push per use), the next to slot 1, etc.
Total cost = sum over sorted frequencies f[i] of f[i] * (i // 8 + 1).

edge_inputs.description: single distinct letter repeated many times
more than 8 distinct, overflow into slot 2
imbalanced frequencies should assign heaviest to cheapest slot
9 distinct letters, ninth spills to cost 2

stress.description: random lowercase string up to 100000 chars
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(size, 100000)
    word = ''.join(chr(ord('a') + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(n))
    return [word]
